menu_title: Respectfully, No -- Disagreeing With a Stakeholder
menu_desc: Say no to power: concern, evidence, downside, alternative
title_sub: Disagreeing With the Person Who Signs Your Budget
ch3_sub: The decision is made, and she does not want a debate
ch5_sub: She wants facts, not feelings -- and she will say so
ch6_sub: Quick fire, three role-plays, and a stakeholder who calls you negative

plan_row.tema: Respectfully, No -- Disagreeing With a Stakeholder
plan_row.foco: Polite disagreement: I see your point, but... + because + What if we...?
plan_row.atividade: Disagreement note + role-play with a stakeholder who does not want to hear it
plan_row.homework: Record a 2-min disagreement: concern, evidence, downside, alternative

heading: The Word You Never
Say
warmup.intro: Last class you negotiated a date, and you closed with this:
warmup.box_title: Today there is nothing to negotiate. She has already decided.
warmup.box_text: Sophia is adding a new vendor to your project next week. You think it is a mistake. Tell her -- and keep her on your side.

goal.d: Three role-plays, and she pushes back

heading: The Language of a Respectful
No
vocab_ch.sub: 8 words to disagree without losing the room

vocab.def: A worry you can explain with facts
vocab.def_pc: a worry you can explain with facts
vocab.ex: I have one concern about the new vendor.

vocab.hint: Another option you bring with you

vocab.def: To make someone believe you, with reasons
vocab.def_pc: to make someone believe you, with reasons
vocab.ex: The data will convince her, not my opinion.

vocab.def: To think something is true without checking
vocab.def_pc: to think something is true without checking
vocab.hint: To think it is true without checking
vocab.ex: Do not assume the team agrees.

vocab.def: Facts that prove what you say
vocab.def_pc: facts that prove what you say
vocab.ex: I have evidence from the last release.

vocab.def: To stop before speaking, because you are unsure
vocab.def_pc: to stop before speaking, because you are unsure
vocab.ex: Do not hesitate. Say it in the meeting.

vocab.def: The bad part of a good idea
vocab.def_pc: the bad part of a good idea
vocab.hint: The bad part of a good idea
vocab.ex: The downside is two extra weeks of testing.

vocab.def: Good enough to pay the price
vocab.def_pc: good enough to pay the price
vocab.hint: Good enough to pay the price
vocab.ex: The risk is real, and it is not worth it.

heading: A Decision You Did Not
Agree With
vocab_check.prompt: Think of a decision at Oracle that you thought was wrong.
vocab_check.sub: Talk for 90 seconds: what was the decision, what was your concern, and did you say it out loud?

heading: Sophia Has Already
Decided
listenings.text: Diogo, quick one before the meeting. I have decided to bring a new vendor into the project. They start on Monday, and they will take the integration work. I know it is late in the project, and I know your team is used to working alone. But the client is nervous, and this makes them feel safe. I am not asking for a debate in the meeting. If you have a concern, bring it to me now, with evidence, and bring me an alternative. What I will not accept is a long list of problems and no solution. And Diogo, do not go quiet in the meeting and complain later.
comp: To bring a new vendor into the project, starting on Monday.
What does she want from Diogo, before the meeting?
A concern with evidence, and an alternative.
Problems with no solution, or silence in the meeting and complaints later.

heading: The Disagreement,
Done Well
listenings.lead: This is the target. Listen, then do it your way.
listenings.text: Sophia, I see your point, and I understand why the client feels safer. But I have one concern, and I want to be direct about it. In March we brought a vendor in during the test phase, and the integration took three extra weeks. That is my evidence. The downside here is the same: a new team needs two weeks just to understand our system, and those two weeks come out of testing. I am not saying no. I would suggest an alternative: give them the reports, not the integration. They start on Monday, the client sees a new team, and my team keeps the part that is risky. If the reports go well, we give them more in July. I think that is worth it. If you still want them on the integration, I will make it work, but I want the extra two weeks in the plan.
comp: In March, a new vendor in the test phase took three extra weeks.
Give the vendor the reports, not the integration.
What does he ask for, if she keeps her decision?
Two extra weeks in the plan.

heading: The Disagreement
Note
artefact.title: One Concern, One Alternative
artefact.reason: Concern: a new vendor in the integration phase costs us testing time
rows: Evidence: March 2026 -- vendor added late, integration took 3 extra weeks
Downside: 2 weeks of onboarding, taken out of the test phase
Alternative: the vendor takes the reports; my team keeps the integration
If the decision stays: I need 2 extra weeks in the plan
artefact.footer: I am not asking you to change your mind today. I am asking you to hear the evidence.

grammar.prompt: Which one softens? Which one proves? Which one offers a way out?

discovery.html: "I see your point, but I have one concern."
discovery.speak: I see your point, but I have one concern.

discovery.html: "I am not sure that will work, because we tried it in March."
discovery.speak: I am not sure that will work, because we tried it in March.

discovery.html: "What if we give them the reports instead?"
discovery.speak: What if we give them the reports instead?

discovery.html: "I would suggest a two-week pilot."
discovery.speak: I would suggest a two-week pilot.

rows: I see your point, but / I am not sure that...
I see your point, but I have one concern.
...because the same thing cost us three weeks in March.
What if we + verb / I would suggest + noun
What if we give them the reports instead?
grammar.note: Never disagree with empty hands. A concern with no evidence is an opinion, and opinions lose to power. A concern with evidence and an alternative is a contribution -- and it makes you look senior, not difficult.
mistakes: I am not agree with you.
I do not agree with you.
I disagree of the new vendor.
I disagree with the new vendor.
grammar.mistake_note: Disagree is a verb -- never "I am not agree". And you disagree with a person or a plan, never "of".

heading: The Five Moves of a Respectful
No
bank.sub: Soften → the concern → the evidence → the downside → the alternative
bank.label: How to disagree and keep the room
items: I see your point, and I understand the client.
But I have one concern about the timing.
In March, the same decision cost us three weeks.
The downside is two weeks of onboarding, taken out of testing.
What if we give them the reports instead?
If you still want the integration, I need two extra weeks in the plan.

scenarios: Your stakeholder wants to cut the test phase in half to hit the date. You think it is dangerous.
A director decides your team will use a new tool in the middle of the project. Nobody knows it.
The client asks for a feature that will break the integration. Your boss says yes before asking you.
scenarios_sub: One concern, one piece of evidence, one alternative. Sixty seconds each.

heading: I Have One
Concern

lines.html: The vendor starts Monday, on the integration. The client is happy. Any problem with that?
lines.speak: The vendor starts Monday, on the integration. The client is happy. Any problem with that?

lines.html: I see your point, and I understand the client. But I have one concern, and I do not want to hesitate about it.
lines.speak: I see your point, and I understand the client. But I have one concern, and I do not want to hesitate about it.

lines.html: Go ahead. But bring me facts, not feelings.
lines.speak: Go ahead. But bring me facts, not feelings.

lines.html: In March we added a vendor during the test phase. Integration took three extra weeks. That is my evidence.
lines.speak: In March we added a vendor during the test phase. Integration took three extra weeks. That is my evidence.

lines.html: That was a different team. Do not assume it will happen again.
lines.speak: That was a different team. Do not assume it will happen again.

lines.html: Fair. But the downside is the same: two weeks of onboarding, and those weeks come out of testing.
lines.speak: Fair. But the downside is the same: two weeks of onboarding, and those weeks come out of testing.

lines.html: So you want me to tell the client no?
lines.speak: So you want me to tell the client no?

lines.html: No. I would suggest an alternative: the vendor takes the reports, my team keeps the integration. The client still sees a new team.
lines.speak: No. I would suggest an alternative: the vendor takes the reports, my team keeps the integration. The client still sees a new team.

lines.html: That might work. Convince me on Friday -- with the March numbers.
lines.speak: That might work. Convince me on Friday, with the March numbers.

comp: Why does Sophia want the vendor on the project?
The client is happy, and the client feels safer with a new team.
What does she ask Diogo to bring?
Facts, not feelings -- and the March numbers on Friday.
Does she accept the alternative immediately?
No. She says it might work, and asks him to convince her on Friday.

quickfire.situation: Your director says: 'This is decided. Any objections?' You have one. Speak.
tips: Soften first: 'I see your point, but...'

quickfire.situation: Say your concern in one sentence, with no adjectives.
tips: 'I have one concern about the timing.'

quickfire.situation: Give the evidence -- a fact from a past project.
tips: A date, a number, a result.

quickfire.situation: Name the downside of her idea, without attacking her.
tips: Attack the plan, never the person.
Make it easy to say yes.

quickfire.situation: She keeps her decision. Close the conversation without losing her.
tips: Ask for what you need to make it work.

items: Are you telling me the client is wrong?
Why did you not raise this three weeks ago?
If I say no to your alternative, will you still support the decision in front of the team?
pressure.followup: Answer first, evidence second, alternative third. Never attack the person -- only the plan.

heading: Say It to Her
Face
guided.prompt: Ten minutes with Sophia. Disagree -- and keep her.
guided.sub: Use the disagreement note. Five moves, in this order:

heading: She Does Not Want to
Hear It
semi.quote: The decision is made. The client already knows.
semi.sub: She pushes back twice: first she says you are being negative, then she says you had time to speak earlier. Hold your ground, stay calm, and end with an alternative she can take to the client.
chips: my concern is not the vendor, it is the phase

heading: Your Real
Disagreement
free.headline: Two minutes. No notes. One concern.
free.sub: Take a real decision at Oracle that you disagree with, today. Say the concern, the evidence, the downside, and the alternative -- to the real person, in your head. End by accepting the decision if it stays.

survival.en: I see your point, but I have one concern.
survival.pt: Entendo seu ponto, mas tenho uma preocupação.

survival.en: I am not sure that will work, because we tried it in March.
survival.pt: Não tenho certeza de que vai funcionar, porque tentamos isso em março.

survival.en: The downside is two weeks of onboarding.
survival.pt: A desvantagem são duas semanas de onboarding.

survival.en: What if we give them the reports instead?
survival.pt: E se dermos os relatórios a eles, em vez disso?

survival.en: I do not agree, but I will support the decision.
survival.pt: Não concordo, mas vou apoiar a decisão.

checks: I can disagree without saying "you are wrong".
I can soften: "I see your point, but..."
I can name the downside of an idea.

closing.line: You disagreed with the person who signs your budget, and she is still listening.
closing.next_desc: Giving difficult feedback to someone on your team

order_audio.text: Sophia announces that a new vendor starts on Monday, on the integration. Diogo softens, and says he has one concern. He gives his evidence: in March, the same decision cost three extra weeks. He names the downside: two weeks of onboarding, taken out of testing. Finally, he offers an alternative: the vendor takes the reports, and his team keeps the integration.
items: He gives his evidence: in March, the same decision cost three extra weeks.
Sophia announces that a new vendor starts on Monday.
He offers an alternative: the vendor takes the reports.
Diogo softens, and says he has one concern.
He names the downside: two weeks of onboarding, taken out of testing.

preclass.desc: Disagree with power and survive it: soften, give evidence, name the downside, offer an alternative.
preclass.context: Sophia has already decided: a new vendor starts on Monday. Diogo does not hesitate, and he does not attack her either. He starts soft: "I see your point, but I have one concern." Then he proves it: "I am not sure that will work, because in March the same decision cost us three extra weeks." That is evidence, not an opinion, and evidence is what will convince her. He names the downside -- two weeks of onboarding, taken out of testing -- and he does not assume she can read his mind. Finally, he offers an alternative: "What if we give them the reports instead?" The risk of losing the test phase is not worth it. Sophia still decides. But now she decides with the facts.
quiz_ctx: Why does Diogo talk about March?
To give evidence: the same decision cost three extra weeks.
To blame the vendor from last year.
I do not agree with you.
I am not agree with you.
What does he do at the end?
He refuses to work on the project.
He tells her she is wrong.
preclass.tip_sub: Discordar sem virar inimigo: suavizar, provar com fato e oferecer saída. / Disagreeing without becoming the enemy.
tip_rows: I see your point, but / I am not sure that...
I see your point, but I have one concern.
What if we + verbo / I would suggest + substantivo
What if we give them the reports instead?
preclass.tip_key: Em inglês, disagree é VERBO: "I do not agree" / "I disagree with you" -- nunca "I am not agree" nem "I disagree of". E regra de ouro: nunca discorde de mãos vazias -- traga evidência e uma alternativa.

blanks.hint: Hint: softens before the disagreement / mas
blanks.phrase: I see your point, but I have one concern.

blanks.hint: Hint: a worry you can explain with facts / preocupação
blanks.phrase: I have one concern about the timing.

blanks.hint: Hint: facts that prove what you say / evidência
blanks.phrase: That is my evidence, not my opinion.

blanks.hint: Hint: the bad part of a good idea / desvantagem
blanks.phrase: The downside is two weeks of onboarding.

blanks.pre: "What if we give them the reports
blanks.hint: Hint: in place of the other option / em vez disso
blanks.phrase: What if we give them the reports instead?

blanks.pre: "The risk is real, and it is not
blanks.hint: Hint: not good enough to pay the price / não vale a pena
blanks.phrase: The risk is real, and it is not worth it.

preclass.speech_intro: The five lines of a respectful no. Record each one. Calm voice, no apology -- you are contributing, not complaining.
quiz_sit: Your director announces a decision you think is wrong. You say:
"I see your point, but I have one concern."
Nothing. You complain to your team later.
She asks why. The strongest answer is:
"Because in March the same decision cost us three extra weeks."
"Because I have a bad feeling about it."
She keeps her decision anyway. You:
Accept it out loud, and ask for the two extra weeks you need.
Say yes, and hope it fails so you are proved right.
Repeat your concern three more times.
think: Take a real decision at Oracle that you disagree with. Speak for two minutes: (1) soften, (2) your concern in one sentence, (3) the evidence -- a fact, a date, a number, (4) the downside, (5) your alternative. Record it.
Now she says: "You are being negative. The decision is made." Answer out loud. Do not repeat your concern -- accept the decision, and ask for exactly what you need to make it work. Record it.

complementary.title: Moneyball (2011)
complementary.desc: Billy Beane disagrees with the old scouts in the meeting room. He does not attack them. He uses data to give a different view.
complementary.tip: Watch the scouting room scene and write down 3 phrases he uses to push back without a fight.

complementary.title: BEP 29c -- Meetings in English: How to Disagree
complementary.desc: This lesson teaches soft ways to disagree in a meeting. It shows the yes-but technique, and how to disagree with a question.
complementary.tip: Listen twice, then read the dialog and repeat each disagreeing phrase out loud.

complementary.title: 14 Ways to Agree and Disagree -- Business English Meetings
complementary.desc: Fourteen real phrases to agree and disagree in business meetings. Some are strong, some are polite and soft.
complementary.tip: Choose 4 polite phrases and use them in your next meeting with a stakeholder.

teacher.title: Abertura (2 min): Compartilhe a tela. Diga: 'Last class you asked for time. Today you do the hardest thing in English: you tell a powerful person that she is wrong -- and you keep her.' Avise que hoje ele fala quase o tempo todo.
teacher.warmup: Warm-up falado (4 min): Toque a frase da Aula 10. Depois a virada: 'This time there is nothing to negotiate. She has decided.' Faca a pergunta do box e fique em silencio 90 segundos. Anote os erros, nao corrija agora.
teacher.goal: Objetivo (1 min): 'Words, one move, and then you disagree with me three times.' Rapido.
teacher.vocab_transition: Transicao vocab (1 min): 'Eight words. Click each card.' O vocabulario e ferramenta -- nao alongue.
teacher.vocab1: Vocab reveal 1-4 (4 min): Pista, tentativa, reveal, audio, uma frase real. CCQ 'concern': 'Is a concern a feeling or something you can explain? (Something you can explain.)' CCQ 'assume': 'If I assume, did I check? (No.)' CCQ 'convince': 'Do facts convince, or does volume? (Facts.)'
teacher.vocab2: Vocab reveal 5-8 (4 min): Mesma dinamica. CCQ 'evidence': 'Is an opinion evidence? (No -- evidence is a fact.)' CCQ 'downside': 'Does a good idea have a downside? (Yes, almost always.)' CCQ 'worth it': 'If it is not worth it, do we do it? (No.)'
teacher.vocab_check: Vocab check falado (4 min): 90 segundos sobre uma decisao real da Oracle com que ele nao concordou. Minimo cinco palavras novas. Voce so escuta e conta. Se travar, aponte um chip -- nunca traduza.
teacher.ch3: Transicao (1 min): 'She is not asking. She is telling.' Crie o desconforto -- e dai que sai a fala.
teacher.listening1: Listening 1 (4 min): 'Sophia already made the decision. Listen to what she WILL accept.' Toque uma vez, sem texto. Perguntas no proximo slide.
teacher.listening1_comp: Comprehension falada (3 min): Respostas em voz alta antes do clique. Depois: 'What does she hate more -- disagreement, or silence?' Deixe ele deduzir falando.
teacher.artefact: Artefato (3 min): Nao leia por ele. 'Read it, then disagree with me out loud, in five moves.' Ele fala; voce marca o que faltou. Este card e o roteiro do role-play guiado.
teacher.grammar: Grammar discovery (4 min): Toque as 4 frases. Pergunta unica: 'Which one softens? Which one proves? Which one offers a way out?' Guie: I see your point, but = suavizar; because + fato = provar; What if we / I would suggest = oferecer.
teacher.rule: Reveal the Rule (3 min): Clique. Tres movimentos: suavizar, provar, oferecer. Exija 2 frases dele com 'because' + fato REAL da Oracle. CCQ: 'Is "I have a bad feeling" evidence? (No.)'
teacher.mistake: Common Mistake (2 min): Dois erros de brasileiro. Peca as versoes certas em voz alta, duas vezes. CCQ: 'I am not agree -- correct? (No: I do not agree.)'
teacher.bank: Useful language falado (4 min): Cada frase lida em voz alta e imediatamente reescrita para uma decisao real dele. Seis frases da tela, seis frases dele. E o esqueleto dos role-plays.
teacher.ch5: Transicao (1 min): 'Now she is in front of you, and she does not want to hear it.' Suba a tensao.
teacher.dialogue: Dialogo (6 min): Voce e a Sophia, e voce reage mal: 'facts, not feelings'. Antes de cada fala do Diogo, ELE fala primeiro; so entao clique e toque o audio. Repare que ele nunca diz 'you are wrong'.
teacher.dialogue_comp: Dialogue comprehension (2 min): Perguntas sobre a SOPHIA. Depois: 'Did he win? Or did he just survive?' Deixe ele defender a resposta -- e a melhor fala da aula.
teacher.listening2: Listening 2 (4 min): 'This is the disagreement, done well -- 60 seconds.' Toque uma vez. Depois: 'What is his evidence? What is his alternative?' -- ele fala.
teacher.scenarios: Scenarios falados (5 min): Tres situacoes, 60 segundos cada, sem preparo. Uma preocupacao, uma evidencia, uma alternativa. Nao corrija no meio.
teacher.ch6: Transicao (1 min): 'From here you disagree, and I push back. Three times.' Daqui ate o fim, ele fala quase sozinho.
teacher.quickfire: Quick Fire (5 min): Uma situacao por vez, resposta imediata. Sem correcao entre elas -- fluencia primeiro. Tips so em ultimo caso.
teacher.rp_guided: Role-play GUIADO (4 min): Voce e a Sophia. Ele discorda usando os cinco movimentos do artefato. Nao interrompa; no fim marque o que faltou. Se ele so reclamar sem alternativa, mande refazer.
teacher.rp_semi: Role-play SEMI-LIVRE (5 min): Agora voce reage mal, duas vezes: 'You are being negative' e depois 'You had three weeks to say this'. Ele tem de manter a calma, nao repetir a preocupacao e fechar com uma alternativa que voce possa levar ao cliente.
teacher.rp_free: Role-play LIVRE (5 min): Sem apoio. 'A real decision at Oracle you disagree with. Two minutes. One concern, one alternative. Go.' NAO interrompa, cronometre e diga o tempo no fim. So entao 2 correcoes.
teacher.pressure: Under pressure (4 min): Tres perguntas duras, uma a uma, sem aviso. Resposta primeiro, evidencia depois. Se hesitar mais de 3 segundos, repita a pergunta.
teacher.survival: Survival Card (3 min): 'Five phrases. With these you can disagree with anyone, at any level.' Toque cada uma; ele repete com tom firme e calmo, nunca defensivo.
teacher.learned: Wrap-up (3 min): Ele clica cada check e diz a frase que prova. So depois devolva as 2 correcoes. Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos discordando de uma decisao real (preocupacao + evidencia + alternativa).
teacher.closing: Fechamento (1 min): Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos discordando de uma decisao real e enviar. Anuncie a Aula 12: 'Next time it is worse -- you give difficult feedback to someone on your team.'
